**Collateral task ID:** `ct-001`

tasks.title: BUG: G7 comments lost when GitHub API rate-limited — need local queue + auto-sync
tasks.description:
## Problème

Lors de rate-limiting GitHub API, les commentaires G7 (échanges user/Claude) ne sont pas postés sur l'issue de session. Les échanges sont perdus — l'issue ne reflète pas la session complète.

## Cause racine

`post_exchange()` et `issue_comment_post()` échouent silencieusement ou lancent une exception quand l'API retourne 403/429 (rate limit). Aucun mécanisme de rétention locale ni de retry différé.

## Solution proposée

1. **File d'attente locale** — quand un commentaire ne peut p

tasks.title: SESSION: Implement recover and recall commands as native code (2026-03-04)
tasks.description:
Implement the two commands defined in the recall/recover split (PR #687):
- **recover**: Python implementation for branch-based work recovery from stranded claude/* branches
- **recall**: Python implementation for deep memory search across all knowledge channels

Both should be callable from session_agent and usable by Claude Code sessions natively.

tasks.title: SESSION: Rename recall to recover + create new hybrid recall as deep memory search (2026-03-04)
tasks.description:
Split current recall (branch recovery) into two commands:
- **recover**: branch-based work recovery (resiliency category) — the current recall functionality
- **recall**: deep memory search across all knowledge channels (remembering category) — new capability
- recall is hybrid: searches memory first, can invoke recover when stranded branch work is found

tasks.title: SESSION: Implement multi-issue notes generation + rescue session completion (2026-03-04)
tasks.description:
Implement per-issue metrics and time compilation in generate_session_notes().

The methodology specs (metrics-compilation.md, time-compilation.md) define multi-issue format but the code only supports single-issue output. This session also closes orphan issue #611 and tracks rescue work.

tasks.title: SESSION: Remove NEWS.md and PLAN.md from wakeup startup reads (2026-03-03)
tasks.description: Update the wakeup protocol to stop reading NEWS.md and PLAN.md during startup steps.

tasks.title: SESSION: Fix G7 enforcement — auto-post all session exchanges to issue (2026-03-04)
tasks.description: Fix the gap where G7 (all exchanges on issue) is documented but not enforced. Currently only action reports are posted — user/Claude conversational exchanges are missed, forcing manual copy.

tasks.title: SESSION: README.md essential file never updated during sessions (2026-03-03)
tasks.description:
README.md is listed as essential file but sessions systematically skip updating it.

Fix: (1) add README.md update check to save protocol gate, (2) add to universal inheritance checklist with explicit enforcement.

tasks.title: SESSION: Multi-request detection in entry prompts (2026-03-03)
tasks.description:
Formalize protocol for detecting multiple distinct requests in a single user prompt.

Currently: add-on detection (v53) classifies mid-work comments. But if the entry prompt contains 2+ distinct requests, the system has no parsing protocol — it treats everything as one task.

Fix: parse entry prompt for multiple action words / distinct requests → create separate issues → sequence in plan.

tasks.title: SESSION: Consistent table column sizing across Session Viewer (2026-03-03)
tasks.description: All grouped key-value tables in Session Viewer must have same first two column widths, adapting to the maximum size. Convention to be documented in methodology.

tasks.description:
The labels marked **(default)** are GitHub's built-in labels that come with every new repo. The rest were created by sessions using the knowledge system.

**GitHub default labels** (7 — came with the repo):
- `bug`, `documentation`, `duplicate`, `enhancement`, `good first issue`, `help wanted`, `invalid`, `question`, `wontfix`

**Created by knowledge sessions** (13):
- `SESSION` — session tracking issues
- `METHODOLOGY:` — methodology-related
- `PUB:` — publication-related
- `analysis` — analysi

tasks.title: feat: Export portrait/paysage pour compilations métriques et temps
tasks.description:
## Objectif

Ajouter la possibilité d'exporter les compilations (métriques et temps) en format portrait OU paysage.

### Contexte
Les tableaux de compilation de métriques et de temps peuvent être larges (nombreuses colonnes). L'export en format paysage permet une meilleure lisibilité des tableaux détaillés, tandis que le portrait convient aux résumés.

### Formats visés
- Letter portrait
- Letter paysage (landscape)
- Legal portrait  
- Legal paysage (landscape)

### Disposition
Le tableau s'ada

tasks.title: SESSION: Méthodologie temps — ventilation entreprise et modèle 3 pointes (2026-02-27)
tasks.description:
## Session Metadata
- **Branch**: claude/document-time-tracking-udFRn
- **Type**: Documentation / Methodology

## Summary
Formaliser la ventilation du temps entreprise (Réalisation / Documentation / Formation) dans la méthodologie de compilation du temps, et corriger le modèle de proportions temps actif vs temps calendrier pour refléter la réalité (pie chart à 3 pointes: machine, humain, calendrier).

tasks.title: SESSION: Session notes (markdown) no longer written during save — runtime cache confused with session notes
tasks.description:
## Bug Description

After the session-runtime cache feature was introduced (v51-v54 evolution), Claude sessions confuse two distinct persistence systems that **must coexist**:

| System | File pattern | Purpose | Consumer |
|--------|-------------|---------|----------|
| **Session notes** (markdown) | `notes/session-YYYY-MM-DD-*.md` | Metrics, time compilations, human-readable session record | `generate_sessions.py` → `sessions.json` → **Session Viewer (I1)** |
| **Runtime cache** (JSON) | `note

tasks.title: FEATURE: Session Runtime Cache — Persistent Session Metadata Across Compaction/Restart
tasks.description:
## Feature: Session Runtime Cache

### Parent
P0 — Knowledge System (#4 board)
Related: #479 (Agent Ticket Sync)

### Problem
After compaction or container restart, the session's tracking issue number is lost. The agent cannot auto-reconnect because the issue # only lived in context memory and the volatile /tmp/ state file.

### Solution
`notes/session-runtime.json` — a persistent cache file that stores:
- Session issue number
- Repository (owner/repo)
- Branch name
- Agent mode (normal/live/bur

tasks.title: HARVEST: g:<board>:<item>[:<action>] — GitHub board item alias convention
tasks.description:
## Summary

New alias convention discovered in knowledge-live (P3) session 2026-02-25.

## Convention

Extends the `#N:` project alias to GitHub board items:

```
g:<board>:<item>           — reference a GitHub board item
g:<board>:<item>:done      — mark item as done (compilation action)
```

| Part | Meaning | Example |
|------|---------|---------|
| `g` | GitHub board scope | — |
| `<board>` | GitHub Project board number | `10` = Export Documentation |
| `<item>` | Item index (1-based, creati

tasks.title: Story's layout convention
tasks.description:

Improve the Success Stories summary pages with a consistent layout convention that includes:

1. **Time delivery paragraph** — Add session time / delivery breakdown text to each story on the summary page (currently only on full/complete pages)
2. **Mermaid pie chart** — Add a pie chart per story showing execution vs calendar time proportions, theme-aware colors with transparent background
3. **Reusable in full pages** — The pie chart convention applies to both summary and full pages

tasks.title: METHODOLOGY: Resiliency Pattern — Client Disconnect Recovery During PR Lifecycle
tasks.description:
## Summary

Observed scenario on branch `claude/extract-image-text-0vJA3`: agent session pushed commits successfully but client interface disconnected before PR creation. Work was preserved on remote — only the PR step needed re-execution.

## Resiliency Pattern Documented

Full documentation written at `knowledge-live/notes/resiliency-client-disconnect-recovery.md` covering four recovery paths:

| Path | Method | Speed | Recommended |
|------|--------|-------|-------------|
| **A** | Browser re

tasks.title: Create documentation project in Knowledge core
tasks.description:
## Description

Create a dedicated project for documentation in the Knowledge core repository. This project should organize and track all documentation efforts across the knowledge system.

## Context

- P16 (Project documentation) exists as a managed project in P3 (knowledge-live), documenting GitHub Project integration methodology
- The core knowledge repo needs its own documentation project to track docs, publications, and content authorship across all satellites
- Should be registered with a
